[\"20160525 13:30:00.128\", \"AAPL\", \"98.62\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.129\", \"AAPL\", \"98.62\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.129\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.129\", \"AAPL\", \"98.62\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.129\", \"AAPL\", \"98.62\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.129\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.130\", \"MSFT\", \"51.93\", \"51.95\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.130\", \"MSFT\", \"51.93\", \"51.95\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.130\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.131\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.131\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.135\", \"MSFT\", \"51.92\", \"51.95\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.135\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.136\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.136\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.144\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.144\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.62\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.60\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.61\", \"98.63\"],\n                [\"20160525 13:30:00.145\", \"AAPL\", \"98.60\", \"98.63\"],\n            ],\n            columns=\"time,ticker,bid,ask\".split(\",\"),\n        )\n        quotes[\"bid\"] = quotes[\"bid\"].astype(\"float64\")\n        quotes[\"ask\"] = quotes[\"ask\"].astype(\"float64\")\n        quotes = self.prep_data(quotes, dedupe=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_basic_no_by(self, trades, asof, quotes):\n        f = (\n            lambda x: x[x.ticker == \"MSFT\"]\n            .drop(\"ticker\", axis=1)\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n\n        # just use a single ticker\n        expected = f(asof)\n        trades = f(trades)\n        quotes = f(quotes)\n\n        result = merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_join_keys(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = r\"incompatible merge keys \\[1\\] .* must be the same type\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, left_on=\"time\", right_on=\"bid\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=[\"time\", \"ticker\"], by=\"ticker\")\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=\"can only asof on a key for left\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, by=\"ticker\")\n\n    def test_with_duplicates(self, datapath, trades, quotes, asof):\n        q = (\n            pd.concat([quotes, quotes])\n            .sort_values([\"time\", \"ticker\"])\n            .reset_index(drop=True)\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(trades, q, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n        expected = self.prep_data(asof)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_with_duplicates_no_on(self):\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [1, 2, 2], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"key\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"key\": [1, 1, 3], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 1, 3]}\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_valid_allow_exact_matches(self, trades, quotes):\n        msg = \"allow_exact_matches must be boolean, passed foo\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", allow_exact_matches=\"foo\"\n            )\n\n    def test_valid_tolerance(self, trades, quotes):\n        # dti\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1s\"))\n\n        # integer\n        merge_asof(\n            trades.reset_index(),\n            quotes.reset_index(),\n            on=\"index\",\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n\n        msg = r\"incompatible tolerance .*, must be compat with type .*\"\n\n        # incompat\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=1)\n\n        # invalid\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=1.0,\n            )\n\n        msg = \"tolerance must be positive\"\n\n        # invalid negative\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=-Timedelta(\"1s\")\n            )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(\n                trades.reset_index(),\n                quotes.reset_index(),\n                on=\"index\",\n                by=\"ticker\",\n                tolerance=-1,\n            )\n\n    def test_non_sorted(self, trades, quotes):\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\", ascending=False)\n\n        # we require that we are already sorted on time & quotes\n        assert not trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"left keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        trades = trades.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert not quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"right keys must be sorted\"):\n            merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n        quotes = quotes.sort_values(\"time\")\n        assert trades.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n        assert quotes.time.is_monotonic_increasing\n\n        # ok, though has dupes\n        merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"tolerance_ts\",\n        [Timedelta(\"1day\"), datetime.timedelta(days=1)],\n        ids=[\"Timedelta\", \"datetime.timedelta\"],\n    )\n    def test_tolerance(self, tolerance_ts, trades, quotes, tolerance):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=tolerance_ts\n        )\n        expected = tolerance\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_nearest(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\", tolerance=1)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_tz(self, unit):\n        # GH 14844\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-02\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value1\": np.arange(5),\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-01\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value2\": list(\"ABCDE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"date\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1 day\"))\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"date\": pd.date_range(\n                    start=to_datetime(\"2016-01-02\"),\n                    freq=\"D\",\n                    periods=5,\n                    tz=pytz.timezone(\"UTC\"),\n                    unit=unit,\n                ),\n                \"value1\": np.arange(5),\n                \"value2\": list(\"BCDEE\"),\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_tolerance_float(self):\n        # GH22981\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1.1, 3.5, 10.9], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1.0, 2.5, 3.3, 7.5, 11.5], \"right_val\": [1.0, 2.5, 3.3, 7.5, 11.5]}\n        )\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1.1, 3.5, 10.9],\n                \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"],\n                \"right_val\": [1, 3.3, np.nan],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\", tolerance=0.5)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_index_tolerance(self, trades, quotes, tolerance):\n        # GH 15135\n        expected = tolerance.set_index(\"time\")\n        trades = trades.set_index(\"time\")\n        quotes = quotes.set_index(\"time\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades,\n            quotes,\n            left_index=True,\n            right_index=True,\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=Timedelta(\"1day\"),\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches(self, trades, quotes, allow_exact_matches):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", allow_exact_matches=False\n        )\n        expected = allow_exact_matches\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [2, 7, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\", allow_exact_matches=False\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_nearest(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [2, 3, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\", allow_exact_matches=False\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance(\n        self, trades, quotes, allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance\n    ):\n        result = merge_asof(\n            trades,\n            quotes,\n            on=\"time\",\n            by=\"ticker\",\n            tolerance=Timedelta(\"100ms\"),\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n        )\n        expected = allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance2(self):\n        # GH 13695\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]), \"username\": [\"bob\"]}\n        )\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.000\", \"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]\n                ),\n                \"version\": [1, 2],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\"],\n                \"version\": [2],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\", allow_exact_matches=False)\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\"],\n                \"version\": [1],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            df1,\n            df2,\n            on=\"time\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n            tolerance=Timedelta(\"10ms\"),\n        )\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime([\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\"],\n                \"version\": [np.nan],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance3(self):\n        # GH 13709\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\", \"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]\n                ),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\", \"charlie\"],\n            }\n        )\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.000\", \"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]\n                ),\n                \"version\": [1, 2],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            df1,\n            df2,\n            on=\"time\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n            tolerance=Timedelta(\"10ms\"),\n        )\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\", \"2016-07-15 13:30:00.030\"]\n                ),\n                \"username\": [\"bob\", \"charlie\"],\n                \"version\": [np.nan, np.nan],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance_forward(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 3, 4, 6, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 3, 4, 6, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [np.nan, 6, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left,\n            right,\n            on=\"a\",\n            direction=\"forward\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_allow_exact_matches_and_tolerance_nearest(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 3, 4, 6, 11], \"right_val\": [1, 3, 4, 7, 11]})\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"right_val\": [np.nan, 4, 11]}\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left,\n            right,\n            on=\"a\",\n            direction=\"nearest\",\n            allow_exact_matches=False,\n            tolerance=1,\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_forward_by(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 5, 10, 12, 15],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"X\", \"Y\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\", \"d\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 6, 11, 15, 16],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"Z\", \"Y\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11, 15, 16],\n            }\n        )\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 5, 10, 12, 15],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"X\", \"Y\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\", \"d\", \"e\"],\n                \"right_val\": [1, np.nan, 11, 15, 16],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", by=\"b\", direction=\"forward\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_nearest_by(self):\n        # GH14887\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 5, 10, 12, 15],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"X\", \"Z\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\", \"d\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 6, 11, 15, 16],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"Z\", \"Z\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11, 15, 16],\n            }\n        )\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"a\": [1, 5, 10, 12, 15],\n                \"b\": [\"X\", \"X\", \"Z\", \"Z\", \"Y\"],\n                \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\", \"d\", \"e\"],\n                \"right_val\": [1, 1, 11, 11, 16],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", by=\"b\", direction=\"nearest\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_by_int(self):\n        # we specialize by type, so test that this is correct\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.030\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.015\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.025\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.035\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.055\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.065\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 1, 3, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"value2\": [2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value2\"],\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"time\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"time\": to_datetime(\n                    [\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.020\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.030\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.040\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.050\",\n                        \"20160525 13:30:00.060\",\n                    ]\n                ),\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 1, 3, 2],\n                \"value1\": [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5],\n                \"value2\": [2.2, 2.1, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7],\n            },\n            columns=[\"time\", \"key\", \"value1\", \"value2\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float(self):\n        # mimics how to determine the minimum-price variation\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"price\": [5.01, 0.0023, 25.13, 340.05, 30.78, 1040.90, 0.0078],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0], \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05]},\n            columns=[\"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BGACEDF\"),\n                \"price\": [0.0023, 0.0078, 5.01, 25.13, 30.78, 340.05, 1040.90],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.05],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79], \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\")},\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": list(\"xxxxxyz\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_specialized_type_by_int(self, any_real_numpy_dtype):\n        # see gh-13936\n        dtype = np.dtype(any_real_numpy_dtype).type\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"value\": [5, 2, 25, 100, 78, 120, 79],\n                \"key\": [1, 2, 3, 2, 3, 1, 2],\n                \"symbol\": list(\"ABCDEFG\"),\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\"],\n        )\n        df1.value = dtype(df1.value)\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"value\": [0, 80, 120, 125], \"key\": [1, 2, 2, 3], \"result\": list(\"xyzw\")},\n            columns=[\"value\", \"key\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        df2.value = dtype(df2.value)\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"value\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"value\", by=\"key\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"BACEGDF\"),\n                \"key\": [2, 1, 3, 3, 2, 2, 1],\n                \"value\": [2, 5, 25, 78, 79, 100, 120],\n                \"result\": [np.nan, \"x\", np.nan, np.nan, np.nan, \"y\", \"x\"],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"key\", \"value\", \"result\"],\n        )\n        expected.value = dtype(expected.value)\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_on_float_by_int(self):\n        # type specialize both \"by\" and \"on\" parameters\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.26,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.2598,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    12.5,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\"],\n        )\n\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"exch\": [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3],\n                \"price\": [0.0, 1.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 100.0, 0.0, 5.0, 1000.0],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.0001, 0.25, 1.0],\n            },\n            columns=[\"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        df1 = df1.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n        df2 = df2.sort_values(\"price\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        result = merge_asof(df1, df2, on=\"price\", by=\"exch\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"symbol\": list(\"AAABBBCCC\"),\n                \"exch\": [3, 2, 1, 3, 1, 2, 1, 2, 3],\n                \"price\": [\n                    3.2598,\n                    3.2599,\n                    3.26,\n                    12.5,\n                    12.58,\n                    12.59,\n                    378.15,\n                    378.2,\n                    378.25,\n                ],\n                \"mpv\": [0.0001, 0.0001, 0.01, 0.25, 0.01, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25],\n            },\n            columns=[\"symbol\", \"exch\", \"price\", \"mpv\"],\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_error_raises(self, using_infer_string):\n        if using_infer_string:\n            msg = \"incompatible merge keys\"\n        else:\n            msg = r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\"\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_datatype_categorical_error_raises(self):\n        msg = (\n            r\"incompatible merge keys \\[0\\] .* both sides category, \"\n            \"but not equal ones\"\n        )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"left_val\": [1, 5, 10], \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])}\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7],\n                \"a\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"X\", \"c\", \"X\", \"b\"]),\n            }\n        )\n\n        with pytest.raises(MergeError, match=msg):\n            merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_merge_groupby_multiple_column_with_categorical_column(self):\n        # GH 16454\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"x\", by=[\"y\", \"z\"])\n        expected = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": [0], \"y\": [0], \"z\": pd.Categorical([0])})\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"func\", [lambda x: x, lambda x: to_datetime(x)], ids=[\"numeric\", \"datetime\"]\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"side\", [\"left\", \"right\"])\n    def test_merge_on_nans(self, func, side):\n        # GH 23189\n        msg = f\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\"\n        nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, np.nan])\n        non_nulls = func([1.0, 5.0, 10.0])\n        df_null = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": nulls, \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n        df = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": non_nulls, \"right_val\": [1, 6, 11]})\n\n        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\n            if side == \"left\":\n                merge_asof(df_null, df, on=\"a\")\n            else:\n                merge_asof(df, df_null, on=\"a\")\n\n    def test_by_nullable(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype, using_infer_string):\n        # Note: this test passes if instead of using pd.array we use\n        #  np.array([np.nan, 1]).  Other than that, I (@jbrockmendel)\n        #  have NO IDEA what the expected behavior is.\n        # TODO(GH#32306): may be relevant to the expected behavior here.\n\n        arr = pd.array([pd.NA, 0, 1], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype)\n        if arr.dtype.kind in [\"i\", \"u\"]:\n            max_val = np.iinfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        else:\n            max_val = np.finfo(arr.dtype.numpy_dtype).max\n        # set value s.t. (at least for integer dtypes) arr._values_for_argsort\n        #  is not an injection\n        arr[2] = max_val\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\", \"Wide\", \"Web\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1, 2, 6],\n                \"value\": [\"b\", \"d\", \"f\"],\n            }\n        )\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": arr,\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\", \"To\", \"You\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2, 4, 6],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n            }\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_by_col_tz_aware(self):\n        # GH 21184\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"on_col\": [2],\n                \"values\": [\"a\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"on_col\": [1],\n                \"values\": [\"b\"],\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=\"by_col\", on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [[pd.Timestamp(\"2018-01-01\", tz=\"UTC\"), 2, \"a\", \"b\"]],\n            columns=[\"by_col\", \"on_col\", \"values_x\", \"values_y\"],\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_by_mixed_tz_aware(self, using_infer_string):\n        # GH 26649\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"by_col2\": [\"HELLO\"],\n                \"on_col\": [2],\n                \"value\": [\"a\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col1\": pd.DatetimeIndex([\"2018-01-01\"]).tz_localize(\"UTC\"),\n                \"by_col2\": [\"WORLD\"],\n                \"on_col\": [1],\n                \"value\": [\"b\"],\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\"], on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            [[pd.Timestamp(\"2018-01-01\", tz=\"UTC\"), \"HELLO\", 2, \"a\"]],\n            columns=[\"by_col1\", \"by_col2\", \"on_col\", \"value_x\"],\n        )\n        expected[\"value_y\"] = np.array([np.nan], dtype=object)\n        if using_infer_string:\n            expected[\"value_y\"] = expected[\"value_y\"].astype(\"string[pyarrow_numpy]\")\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"dtype\", [\"float64\", \"int16\", \"m8[ns]\", \"M8[us]\"])\n    def test_by_dtype(self, dtype):\n        # GH 55453, GH 22794\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": np.array([1], dtype=dtype),\n                \"on_col\": [2],\n                \"value\": [\"a\"],\n            }\n        )\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": np.array([1], dtype=dtype),\n                \"on_col\": [1],\n                \"value\": [\"b\"],\n            }\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, by=\"by_col\", on=\"on_col\")\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"by_col\": np.array([1], dtype=dtype),\n                \"on_col\": [2],\n                \"value_x\": [\"a\"],\n                \"value_y\": [\"b\"],\n            }\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_timedelta_tolerance_nearest(self, unit):\n        # GH 27642\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would \"\n                \"round left['time']\"\n            )\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\"],\n        )\n\n        left[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(left[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        right = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(zip([0, 3, 9, 12, 15, 18], [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        right[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(right[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            list(\n                zip(\n                    [0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25],\n                    [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],\n                    [0, np.nan, 2, 4, np.nan, np.nan],\n                )\n            ),\n            columns=[\"time\", \"left\", \"right\"],\n        )\n\n        expected[\"time\"] = pd.to_timedelta(expected[\"time\"], \"ms\").astype(f\"m8[{unit}]\")\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left, right, on=\"time\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1ms\"), direction=\"nearest\"\n        )\n\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_int_type_tolerance(self, any_int_dtype):\n        # GH #28870\n\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [0, 10, 20], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [5, 15, 25], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n        left[\"a\"] = left[\"a\"].astype(any_int_dtype)\n        right[\"a\"] = right[\"a\"].astype(any_int_dtype)\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"a\": [0, 10, 20], \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [np.nan, 1.0, 2.0]}\n        )\n        expected[\"a\"] = expected[\"a\"].astype(any_int_dtype)\n\n        result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", tolerance=10)\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_merge_index_column_tz(self):\n        # GH 29864\n        index = pd.date_range(\"2019-10-01\", freq=\"30min\", periods=5, tz=\"UTC\")\n        left = pd.DataFrame([0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.6], columns=[\"xyz\"], index=index[1:])\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"from_date\": index, \"abc\": [2.46] * 4 + [2.19]})\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left=left, right=right, left_index=True, right_on=[\"from_date\"]\n        )\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"xyz\": [0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.6],\n                \"from_date\": index[1:],\n                \"abc\": [2.46] * 3 + [2.19],\n            },\n            index=pd.date_range(\n                \"2019-10-01 00:30:00\", freq=\"30min\", periods=4, tz=\"UTC\"\n            ),\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n        result = merge_asof(\n            left=right, right=left, right_index=True, left_on=[\"from_date\"]\n        )\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\n                \"from_date\": index,\n                \"abc\": [2.46] * 4 + [2.19],\n                \"xyz\": [np.nan, 0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.6],\n            },\n            index=Index([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]),\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    def test_left_index_right_index_tolerance(self, unit):\n        # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/35558\n        if unit == \"s\":\n            pytest.skip(\n                \"This test is invalid with unit='s' because that would round dr1\"\n            )\n\n        dr1 = pd.date_range(\n            start=\"1/1/2020\", end=\"1/20/2020\", freq=\"2D\", unit=unit\n        ) + Timedelta(seconds=0.4).as_unit(unit)\n        dr2 = pd.date_range(start=\"1/1/2020\", end=\"2/1/2020\", unit=unit)\n\n        df1 = pd.DataFrame({\"val1\": \"foo\"}, index=pd.DatetimeIndex(dr1))\n        df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"val2\": \"bar\"}, index=pd.DatetimeIndex(dr2))\n\n        expected = pd.DataFrame(\n            {\"val1\": \"foo\", \"val2\": \"bar\"}, index=pd.DatetimeIndex(dr1)\n        )\n        result = merge_asof(\n            df1,\n            df2,\n            left_index=True,\n            right_index=True,\n            tolerance=Timedelta(seconds=0.5),\n        )\n        tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"infer_string\", [False, pytest.param(True, marks=td.skip_if_no(\"pyarrow\"))]\n)\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"kwargs\", [{\"on\": \"x\"}, {\"left_index\": True, \"right_index\": True}]\n)\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"data\",\n    [[\"2019-06-01 00:09:12\", \"2019-06-01 00:10:29\"], [1.0, \"2019-06-01 00:10:29\"]],\n)\ndef test_merge_asof_non_numerical_dtype(kwargs, data, infer_string):\n    # GH#29130\n    with option_context(\"future.infer_string\", infer_string):\n        left = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": data}, index=data)\n        right = pd.DataFrame({\"x\": data}, index=data)\n        with pytest.raises(\n            MergeError,\n            match=r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\",\n        ):\n            merge_asof(left, right, **kwargs)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_non_numerical_dtype_object():\n    # GH#29130\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [\"12\", \"13\", \"15\"], \"left_val1\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"left_val\": [\"d\", \"e\", \"f\"]})\n    with pytest.raises(\n        MergeError,\n        match=r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\",\n    ):\n        merge_asof(\n            left,\n            right,\n            left_on=\"left_val1\",\n            right_on=\"a\",\n            left_by=\"a\",\n            right_by=\"left_val\",\n        )\n\n\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"kwargs\",\n    [\n        {\"right_index\": True, \"left_index\": True},\n        {\"left_on\": \"left_time\", \"right_index\": True},\n        {\"left_index\": True, \"right_on\": \"right\"},\n    ],\n)\ndef test_merge_asof_index_behavior(kwargs):\n    # GH 33463\n    index = Index([1, 5, 10], name=\"test\")\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"left\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"left_time\": [1, 4, 10]}, index=index)\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"right\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]}, index=[1, 2, 3, 6, 7])\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, **kwargs)\n\n    expected = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\"left\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"], \"left_time\": [1, 4, 10], \"right\": [1, 3, 7]},\n        index=index,\n    )\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_numeric_column_in_index():\n    # GH#34488\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"b\": [10, 11, 12]}, index=Index([1, 2, 3], name=\"a\"))\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"c\": [20, 21, 22]}, index=Index([0, 2, 3], name=\"a\"))\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"a\", right_on=\"a\")\n    expected = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3], \"b\": [10, 11, 12], \"c\": [20, 21, 22]})\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_numeric_column_in_multiindex():\n    # GH#34488\n    left = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\"b\": [10, 11, 12]},\n        index=pd.MultiIndex.from_arrays([[1, 2, 3], [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]], names=[\"a\", \"z\"]),\n    )\n    right = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\"c\": [20, 21, 22]},\n        index=pd.MultiIndex.from_arrays([[1, 2, 3], [\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]], names=[\"a\", \"y\"]),\n    )\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"a\", right_on=\"a\")\n    expected = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3], \"b\": [10, 11, 12], \"c\": [20, 21, 22]})\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_numeri_column_in_index_object_dtype():\n    # GH#34488\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"b\": [10, 11, 12]}, index=Index([\"1\", \"2\", \"3\"], name=\"a\"))\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"c\": [20, 21, 22]}, index=Index([\"m\", \"n\", \"o\"], name=\"a\"))\n\n    with pytest.raises(\n        MergeError,\n        match=r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\",\n    ):\n        merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"a\", right_on=\"a\")\n\n    left = left.reset_index().set_index([\"a\", \"b\"])\n    right = right.reset_index().set_index([\"a\", \"c\"])\n\n    with pytest.raises(\n        MergeError,\n        match=r\"Incompatible merge dtype, .*, both sides must have numeric dtype\",\n    ):\n        merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"a\", right_on=\"a\")\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_array_as_on(unit):\n    # GH#42844\n    dti = pd.DatetimeIndex(\n        [\"2021/01/01 00:37\", \"2021/01/01 01:40\"], dtype=f\"M8[{unit}]\"\n    )\n    right = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"a\": [2, 6],\n            \"ts\": dti,\n        }\n    )\n    ts_merge = pd.date_range(\n        start=pd.Timestamp(\"2021/01/01 00:00\"), periods=3, freq=\"1h\", unit=unit\n    )\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"b\": [4, 8, 7]})\n    result = merge_asof(\n        left,\n        right,\n        left_on=ts_merge,\n        right_on=\"ts\",\n        allow_exact_matches=False,\n        direction=\"backward\",\n    )\n    expected = pd.DataFrame({\"b\": [4, 8, 7], \"a\": [np.nan, 2, 6], \"ts\": ts_merge})\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n    result = merge_asof(\n        right,\n        left,\n        left_on=\"ts\",\n        right_on=ts_merge,\n        allow_exact_matches=False,\n        direction=\"backward\",\n    )\n    expected = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"a\": [2, 6],\n            \"ts\": dti,\n            \"b\": [4, 8],\n        }\n    )\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_raise_for_duplicate_columns():\n    # GH#50102\n    left = pd.DataFrame([[1, 2, \"a\"]], columns=[\"a\", \"a\", \"left_val\"])\n    right = pd.DataFrame([[1, 1, 1]], columns=[\"a\", \"a\", \"right_val\"])\n\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"column label 'a'\"):\n        merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"column label 'a'\"):\n        merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"a\", right_on=\"right_val\")\n\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"column label 'a'\"):\n        merge_asof(left, right, left_on=\"left_val\", right_on=\"a\")\n\n\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"dtype\",\n    [\n        \"Int64\",\n        pytest.param(\"int64[pyarrow]\", marks=td.skip_if_no(\"pyarrow\")),\n        pytest.param(\"timestamp[s][pyarrow]\", marks=td.skip_if_no(\"pyarrow\")),\n    ],\n)\ndef test_merge_asof_extension_dtype(dtype):\n    # GH 52904\n    left = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"join_col\": [1, 3, 5],\n            \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3],\n        }\n    )\n    right = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"join_col\": [2, 3, 4],\n            \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3],\n        }\n    )\n    left = left.astype({\"join_col\": dtype})\n    right = right.astype({\"join_col\": dtype})\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"join_col\")\n    expected = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"join_col\": [1, 3, 5],\n            \"left_val\": [1, 2, 3],\n            \"right_val\": [np.nan, 2.0, 3.0],\n        }\n    )\n    expected = expected.astype({\"join_col\": dtype})\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\n@td.skip_if_no(\"pyarrow\")\ndef test_merge_asof_pyarrow_td_tolerance():\n    # GH 56486\n    ser = pd.Series(\n        [datetime.datetime(2023, 1, 1)], dtype=\"timestamp[us, UTC][pyarrow]\"\n    )\n    df = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"timestamp\": ser,\n            \"value\": [1],\n        }\n    )\n    result = merge_asof(df, df, on=\"timestamp\", tolerance=Timedelta(\"1s\"))\n    expected = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"timestamp\": ser,\n            \"value_x\": [1],\n            \"value_y\": [1],\n        }\n    )\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_read_only_ndarray():\n    # GH 53513\n    left = pd.Series([2], index=[2], name=\"left\")\n    right = pd.Series([1], index=[1], name=\"right\")\n    # set to read-only\n    left.index.values.flags.writeable = False\n    right.index.values.flags.writeable = False\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n    expected = pd.DataFrame({\"left\": [2], \"right\": [1]}, index=[2])\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_multiby_with_categorical():\n    # GH 43541\n    left = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"c1\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"a\", \"b\", \"b\"], categories=[\"a\", \"b\"]),\n            \"c2\": [\"x\"] * 4,\n            \"t\": [1] * 4,\n            \"v\": range(4),\n        }\n    )\n    right = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"c1\": pd.Categorical([\"b\", \"b\"], categories=[\"b\", \"a\"]),\n            \"c2\": [\"x\"] * 2,\n            \"t\": [1, 2],\n            \"v\": range(2),\n        }\n    )\n    result = merge_asof(\n        left,\n        right,\n        by=[\"c1\", \"c2\"],\n        on=\"t\",\n        direction=\"forward\",\n        suffixes=[\"_left\", \"_right\"],\n    )\n    expected = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"c1\": pd.Categorical([\"a\", \"a\", \"b\", \"b\"], categories=[\"a\", \"b\"]),\n            \"c2\": [\"x\"] * 4,\n            \"t\": [1] * 4,\n            \"v_left\": range(4),\n            \"v_right\": [np.nan, np.nan, 0.0, 0.0],\n        }\n    )\n    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, expected)\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_indicator():\n    # Test that indicator parameter works with merge_asof\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n\n    # Check that _merge column exists\n    assert \"_merge\" in result.columns\n    assert result[\"_merge\"].dtype.name == \"category\"\n\n    # For asof merge with default backward direction, all rows should match\n    # First row (a=1) matches exactly, so it's \"both\"\n    # Second row (a=5) has a match (a=3), so it's \"both\"\n    # Third row (a=10) has a match (a=7), so it's \"both\"\n    assert (result[\"_merge\"] == \"both\").all()\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name():\n    # Test that indicator parameter works with custom name\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"custom_merge\")\n\n    # Check that custom_merge column exists\n    assert \"custom_merge\" in result.columns\n    assert result[\"custom_merge\"].dtype.name == \"category\"\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches():\n    # Test indicator when some rows don't have matches\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [0.5, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3]})\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n\n    # Check that _merge column exists\n    assert \"_merge\" in result.columns\n\n    # First row (a=0.5) has no match (min in right is 1)\n    # Second row (a=5) matches with a=3 (backward direction)\n    # Third row (a=10) matches with a=3 (backward direction)\n    assert result.iloc[0][\"_merge\"] == \"left_only\"\n    assert result.iloc[1][\"_merge\"] == \"both\"\n    assert result.iloc[2][\"_merge\"] == \"both\"\n\n\ndef test_merge_asof_indicator_false():\n    # Test that indicator=False doesn't add _merge column\n    left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n    right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n\n    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=False)\n\n    # Check that _merge column does not exist\n    assert \"_merge\" not in result.columns\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        "     # Check that _merge column does not exist",
        "     assert \"_merge\" not in result.columns",
        "+",
        "+",
        "+def test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by():",
        "+    # Test indicator works with by parameter",
        "+    left = pd.DataFrame(",
        "+        {",
        "+            \"time\": pd.to_datetime([\"2016-05-25 13:30:00\", \"2016-05-25 13:30:01\"]),",
        "+            \"ticker\": [\"MSFT\", \"AAPL\"],",
        "+            \"left_val\": [100, 200],",
        "+        }",
        "+    )",
        "+    right = pd.DataFrame(",
        "+        {",
        "+            \"time\": pd.to_datetime([\"2016-05-25 13:30:00\", \"2016-05-25 13:30:02\"]),",
        "+            \"ticker\": [\"MSFT\", \"AAPL\"],",
        "+            \"right_val\": [10, 20],",
        "+        }",
        "+    )",
        "+",
        "+    result = merge_asof(left, right, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", indicator=True)",
        "+",
        "+    # Check that _merge column exists",
        "+    assert \"_merge\" in result.columns",
        "+    assert result[\"_merge\"].dtype.name == \"category\""

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    @final\n    @cache_readonly\n    def _indicator_name(self) -> str | None:\n        if isinstance(self.indicator, str):\n            return self.indicator\n        elif isinstance(self.indicator, bool):\n            return \"_merge\" if self.indicator else None\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"indicator option can only accept boolean or string arguments\"\n            )\n\n    @final\n    def _indicator_pre_merge(\n        self, left: DataFrame, right: DataFrame",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
    "numLines": 15,
    "startLine": 908,
    "totalLines": 2793
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nIntegration test for merge_asof indicator support.\nThis tests the actual implementation without needing pytest installed.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\n\n# This is a minimal test to check the implementation structure\ndef main():\n    print(\"=\" * 60)\n    print(\"Integration Test for merge_asof indicator\")\n    print(\"=\" * 60)\n\n    try:\n        # Try to import and inspect the modules\n        print(\"\\n1. Attempting to parse merge.py...\")\n        with open(\"/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py\", \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n\n        # Check that indicator parameter exists in merge_asof\n        if \"def merge_asof(\" in content and \"indicator: str | bool = False,\" in content:\n            print(\"\u2713 merge_asof has indicator parameter\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 merge_asof missing indicator parameter\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check that indicator is passed to _AsOfMerge\n        if \"indicator=indicator,\" in content:\n            print(\"\u2713 indicator is passed to _AsOfMerge\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 indicator not passed to _AsOfMerge\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check that _AsOfMerge.__init__ has indicator parameter\n        if \"def __init__\" in content and \"indicator: str | bool = False,\" in content:\n            print(\"\u2713 _AsOfMerge.__init__ has indicator parameter\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 _AsOfMerge.__init__ missing indicator parameter\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check that _AsOfMerge.get_result is defined\n        if \"def get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True)\" in content:\n            # Count how many times this appears\n            count = content.count(\"def get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True)\")\n            if count >= 2:  # One in _MergeOperation and one in _AsOfMerge\n                print(f\"\u2713 get_result method is overridden (found {count} definitions)\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"\u2717 get_result not properly overridden (found {count} definitions)\")\n                return 1\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 get_result method pattern not found\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check for _indicator_pre_merge and _indicator_post_merge calls in _AsOfMerge.get_result\n        asof_merge_section = content[content.find(\"class _AsOfMerge\"):content.find(\"def _get_multiindex_indexer\")]\n        if \"_indicator_pre_merge\" in asof_merge_section and \"_indicator_post_merge\" in asof_merge_section:\n            print(\"\u2713 _AsOfMerge.get_result calls indicator methods\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 _AsOfMerge.get_result missing indicator handling\")\n            return 1\n\n        print(\"\\n2. Checking test file...\")\n        with open(\"/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py\", \"r\") as f:\n            test_content = f.read()\n\n        test_functions = [\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_false\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by\"\n        ]\n\n        for test_func in test_functions:\n            if f\"def {test_func}(\" in test_content:\n                print(f\"\u2713 {test_func} found\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"\u2717 {test_func} not found\")\n                return 1\n\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n        print(\"\u2713 All integration checks passed!\")\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        return 0\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Error during integration test: {e}\")\n        import traceback\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        return 1\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test_integration.py"
}

  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nIntegration test for merge_asof indicator support.\nThis tests the actual implementation without needing pytest installed.\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\n\n# This is a minimal test to check the implementation structure\ndef main():\n    print(\"=\" * 60)\n    print(\"Integration Test for merge_asof indicator\")\n    print(\"=\" * 60)\n\n    try:\n        # Try to import and inspect the modules\n        print(\"\\n1. Attempting to parse merge.py...\")\n        with open(\"/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py\", \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n\n        # Check that indicator parameter exists in merge_asof\n        if \"def merge_asof(\" in content and \"indicator: str | bool = False,\" in content:\n            print(\"\u2713 merge_asof has indicator parameter\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 merge_asof missing indicator parameter\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check that indicator is passed to _AsOfMerge\n        if \"indicator=indicator,\" in content:\n            print(\"\u2713 indicator is passed to _AsOfMerge\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 indicator not passed to _AsOfMerge\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check that _AsOfMerge.get_result is defined\n        if \"def get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True)\" in content:\n            # Count how many times this appears\n            count = content.count(\"def get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True)\")\n            if count >= 2:  # One in _MergeOperation and one in _AsOfMerge\n                print(f\"\u2713 get_result method is overridden (found {count} definitions)\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"\u2717 get_result not properly overridden (found {count} definitions)\")\n                return 1\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 get_result method pattern not found\")\n            return 1\n\n        # Check for _indicator_pre_merge and _indicator_post_merge calls in _AsOfMerge.get_result\n        asof_merge_section = content[content.find(\"class _AsOfMerge\"):content.find(\"def _get_multiindex_indexer\")]\n        if \"_indicator_pre_merge\" in asof_merge_section and \"_indicator_post_merge\" in asof_merge_section:\n            print(\"\u2713 _AsOfMerge.get_result calls indicator methods\")\n        else:\n            print(\"\u2717 _AsOfMerge.get_result missing indicator handling\")\n            return 1\n\n        print(\"\\n2. Checking test file...\")\n        with open(\"/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py\", \"r\") as f:\n            test_content = f.read()\n\n        test_functions = [\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_false\",\n            \"test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by\"\n        ]\n\n        for test_func in test_functions:\n            if f\"def {test_func}(\" in test_content:\n                print(f\"\u2713 {test_func} found\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"\u2717 {test_func} not found\")\n                return 1\n\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n        print(\"\u2713 All integration checks passed!\")\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        return 0\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Error during integration test: {e}\")\n        import traceback\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        return 1\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",

76. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Implementation Summary: indicator Parameter for merge_asof()\n\n## Overview\nAdded support for the `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` function, following the same pattern as `pd.merge()`. The indicator column shows which table each row originated from ('both', 'left_only', or 'right_only').\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### 1. Modified `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`\n\n#### 1.1 Updated `merge_asof()` function signature (line 440-456)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Purpose**: Allows users to request an indicator column showing merge source\n\n#### 1.2 Updated `merge_asof()` docstring (line 514-517)\n- **Added documentation**: Describes the indicator parameter, its values, and usage with custom names\n\n#### 1.3 Updated `merge_asof()` function call to `_AsOfMerge` (line 712)\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to the `_AsOfMerge` constructor\n\n#### 1.4 Updated `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1903-1915)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_MergeOperation.__init__()` (line 1930)\n\n#### 1.5 Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1970-1987)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` (line 2020)\n\n#### 1.6 Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method (lines 2309-2327)\n- **Implementation**: Overrides parent's `get_result()` to handle indicator processing\n- **Logic**:\n  1. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` to add temporary indicator columns\n  2. Performs the join operation via `_get_join_info()`\n  3. Concatenates results\n  4. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_post_merge()` to create final indicator column\n  5. Adds join keys and restores index levels\n  6. Returns finalized result\n- **Inherited methods used**:\n  - `_indicator_pre_merge()`: Adds _left_indicator and _right_indicator columns\n  - `_indicator_post_merge()`: Creates final indicator column with categorical values\n  - `_reindex_and_concat()`: Handles reindexing and concatenation\n  - `_maybe_add_join_keys()`: Manages join key columns\n  - `_maybe_restore_index_levels()`: Restores index levels if needed\n\n### 2. Modified `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\n#### 2.1 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator()`\n- Tests basic indicator functionality with `indicator=True`\n- Verifies _merge column exists and has categorical dtype\n- Checks that matched rows are marked as \"both\"\n\n#### 2.2 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name()`\n- Tests indicator with custom column name: `indicator=\"custom_merge\"`\n- Verifies custom column exists with categorical dtype\n\n#### 2.3 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches()`\n- Tests indicator when some left rows don't have matches in right\n- Verifies \"left_only\" values for unmatched rows\n- Verifies \"both\" values for matched rows\n\n#### 2.4 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`\n- Tests default behavior: `indicator=False`\n- Verifies _merge column is NOT created when indicator=False\n\n#### 2.5 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by()`\n- Tests indicator works with the `by` parameter\n- Verifies indicator column is created even with grouping\n\n## Design Decisions\n\n### 1. Inheritance Pattern\n- Leveraged existing `_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()` methods from `_MergeOperation`\n- No code duplication; reuses proven indicator handling logic\n\n### 2. Method Override\n- Overrode `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` rather than modifying `_OrderedMerge.get_result()`\n- This allows _AsOfMerge to use indicator-aware logic while keeping _OrderedMerge unchanged\n- Maintains separation of concerns and reduces impact on existing code\n\n### 3. Parameter Passing\n- Updated both `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` and `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept indicator\n- This ensures indicator can be used with merge_ordered in the future if needed\n\n### 4. Validation\n- Leverages `_indicator_name` property from `_MergeOperation` for validation\n- Automatically validates that indicator is bool or str\n- Raises ValueError for invalid types\n\n## Behavior\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=True\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Result includes '_merge' column with values:\n# 0: 'both'      (matched with a=1)\n# 1: 'both'      (matched with a=3)\n# 2: 'both'      (matched with a=7)\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=\"custom_col\"\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"custom_col\")\n# Result includes 'custom_col' instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=False (default)\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n# No indicator column added\n```\n\n## Compatibility\n\n- **Backwards compatible**: Default value is `indicator=False`, maintaining existing behavior\n- **Consistent with merge()**: Uses same indicator semantics and values as `pd.merge()`\n- **Works with all merge_asof parameters**: Compatible with `by`, `tolerance`, `direction`, etc.\n\n## Testing\n\nAll test cases cover:\n- Basic indicator functionality\n- Custom column names\n- Handling of unmatched rows\n- Default behavior (no indicator)\n- Interaction with `by` parameter\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` - Core implementation\n2. `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py` - Test cases\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Implementation Summary: indicator Parameter for merge_asof()\n\n## Overview\nAdded support for the `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` function, following the same pattern as `pd.merge()`. The indicator column shows which table each row originated from ('both', 'left_only', or 'right_only').\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### 1. Modified `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`\n\n#### 1.1 Updated `merge_asof()` function signature (line 440-456)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Purpose**: Allows users to request an indicator column showing merge source\n\n#### 1.2 Updated `merge_asof()` docstring (line 514-517)\n- **Added documentation**: Describes the indicator parameter, its values, and usage with custom names\n\n#### 1.3 Updated `merge_asof()` function call to `_AsOfMerge` (line 712)\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to the `_AsOfMerge` constructor\n\n#### 1.4 Updated `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1903-1915)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_MergeOperation.__init__()` (line 1930)\n\n#### 1.5 Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` signature (line 1970-1987)\n- **Added parameter**: `indicator: str | bool = False`\n- **Change**: Passes `indicator=indicator` to parent `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` (line 2020)\n\n#### 1.6 Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method (lines 2309-2327)\n- **Implementation**: Overrides parent's `get_result()` to handle indicator processing\n- **Logic**:\n  1. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` to add temporary indicator columns\n  2. Performs the join operation via `_get_join_info()`\n  3. Concatenates results\n  4. If indicator is set, calls `_indicator_post_merge()` to create final indicator column\n  5. Adds join keys and restores index levels\n  6. Returns finalized result\n- **Inherited methods used**:\n  - `_indicator_pre_merge()`: Adds _left_indicator and _right_indicator columns\n  - `_indicator_post_merge()`: Creates final indicator column with categorical values\n  - `_reindex_and_concat()`: Handles reindexing and concatenation\n  - `_maybe_add_join_keys()`: Manages join key columns\n  - `_maybe_restore_index_levels()`: Restores index levels if needed\n\n### 2. Modified `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`\n\n#### 2.1 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator()`\n- Tests basic indicator functionality with `indicator=True`\n- Verifies _merge column exists and has categorical dtype\n- Checks that matched rows are marked as \"both\"\n\n#### 2.2 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_custom_name()`\n- Tests indicator with custom column name: `indicator=\"custom_merge\"`\n- Verifies custom column exists with categorical dtype\n\n#### 2.3 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_no_matches()`\n- Tests indicator when some left rows don't have matches in right\n- Verifies \"left_only\" values for unmatched rows\n- Verifies \"both\" values for matched rows\n\n#### 2.4 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_false()`\n- Tests default behavior: `indicator=False`\n- Verifies _merge column is NOT created when indicator=False\n\n#### 2.5 Added test: `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by()`\n- Tests indicator works with the `by` parameter\n- Verifies indicator column is created even with grouping\n\n## Design Decisions\n\n### 1. Inheritance Pattern\n- Leveraged existing `_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()` methods from `_MergeOperation`\n- No code duplication; reuses proven indicator handling logic\n\n### 2. Method Override\n- Overrode `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` rather than modifying `_OrderedMerge.get_result()`\n- This allows _AsOfMerge to use indicator-aware logic while keeping _OrderedMerge unchanged\n- Maintains separation of concerns and reduces impact on existing code\n\n### 3. Parameter Passing\n- Updated both `_OrderedMerge.__init__()` and `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept indicator\n- This ensures indicator can be used with merge_ordered in the future if needed\n\n### 4. Validation\n- Leverages `_indicator_name` property from `_MergeOperation` for validation\n- Automatically validates that indicator is bool or str\n- Raises ValueError for invalid types\n\n## Behavior\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=True\n```python\nleft = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\nright = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=True)\n# Result includes '_merge' column with values:\n# 0: 'both'      (matched with a=1)\n# 1: 'both'      (matched with a=3)\n# 2: 'both'      (matched with a=7)\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=\"custom_col\"\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", indicator=\"custom_col\")\n# Result includes 'custom_col' instead of '_merge'\n```\n\n### merge_asof with indicator=False (default)\n```python\nresult = pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n# No indicator column added\n```\n\n## Compatibility\n\n- **Backwards compatible**: Default value is `indicator=False`, maintaining existing behavior\n- **Consistent with merge()**: Uses same indicator semantics and values as `pd.merge()`\n- **Works with all merge_asof parameters**: Compatible with `by`, `tolerance`, `direction`, etc.\n\n## Testing\n\nAll test cases cover:\n- Basic indicator functionality\n- Custom column names\n- Handling of unmatched rows\n- Default behavior (no indicator)\n- Interaction with `by` parameter\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` - Core implementation\n2. `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py` - Test cases\n",

78. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 921
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    def _indicator_pre_merge(\n        self, left: DataFrame, right: DataFrame\n    ) -> tuple[DataFrame, DataFrame]:\n        columns = left.columns.union(right.columns)\n\n        for i in [\"_left_indicator\", \"_right_indicator\"]:\n            if i in columns:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"Cannot use `indicator=True` option when \"\n                    f\"data contains a column named {i}\"\n                )\n        if self._indicator_name in columns:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column\"\n            )\n\n        left = left.copy()\n        right = right.copy()\n\n        left[\"_left_indicator\"] = 1\n        left[\"_left_indicator\"] = left[\"_left_indicator\"].astype(\"int8\")\n\n        right[\"_right_indicator\"] = 2\n        right[\"_right_indicator\"] = right[\"_right_indicator\"].astype(\"int8\")\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 921,
    "totalLines": 2793
  },
  "type": "text"
}
79. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -A5 \"fill_method\" /workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py | head -20",
  "description": "Check fill_method handling"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    fill_method: str | None = None,\n    suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n    how: JoinHow = \"outer\",\n) -> DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Perform a merge for ordered data with optional filling/interpolation.\n--\n    fill_method : {'ffill', None}, default None\n        Interpolation method for data.\n    suffixes : list-like, default is (\"_x\", \"_y\")\n        A length-2 sequence where each element is optionally a string\n        indicating the suffix to add to overlapping column names in\n        `left` and `right` respectively. Pass a value of `None` instead\n--\n    >>> merge_ordered(df1, df2, fill_method=\"ffill\", left_by=\"group\")\n      key  lvalue group  rvalue\n    0   a       1     a     NaN\n    1   b       1     a     1.0\n    2   c       2     a     2.0\n    3   d       2     a     3.0"
}

}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "            )\n            raise MergeError(msg)\n\n        _OrderedMerge.__init__(\n            self,\n            left,\n            right,\n            on=on,\n            left_on=left_on,\n            right_on=right_on,\n            left_index=left_index,\n            right_index=right_index,\n            how=how,\n            suffixes=suffixes,\n            fill_method=None,\n            indicator=indicator,\n        )\n\n    def _validate_left_right_on(self, left_on, right_on):\n        left_on, right_on = super()._validate_left_right_on(left_on, right_on)\n\n        # we only allow on to be a single item for on\n        if len(left_on) != 1 and not self.left_index:\n            raise MergeError(\"can only asof on a key for left\")\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 2005,
    "totalLines": 2793
  },
  "type": "text"

81. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
  "limit": 30,
  "offset": 737
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    indicator: str | bool\n    validate: str | None\n    join_names: list[Hashable]\n    right_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\n    left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        left: DataFrame | Series,\n        right: DataFrame | Series,\n        how: JoinHow | Literal[\"asof\"] = \"inner\",\n        on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n        left_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n        right_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n        left_index: bool = False,\n        right_index: bool = False,\n        sort: bool = True,\n        suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n        indicator: str | bool = False,\n        validate: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        _left = _validate_operand(left)\n        _right = _validate_operand(right)\n        self.left = self.orig_left = _left\n        self.right = self.orig_right = _right\n        self.how = how\n\n        self.on = com.maybe_make_list(on)\n\n        self.suffixes = suffixes",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 737,
    "totalLines": 2793
  },
  "type": "text"
}
